source_code:
# source: https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic/issues/3859
# repo: pydantic/pydantic
# title: `create_model_from_typeddict` doesn't play nice with `NotRequired`
# 
# ### Checks
# * [ x] I added a descriptive title to this issue
# * [ x] I have searched (google, github) for similar issues and couldn't find anything
# * [ x] I have read and followed [the docs](https://pydantic-docs.helpmanual.io/) and still think this is a bug
# <!-- Sorry to sound so draconian, but every second saved replying to issues is time spend improving pydantic :-) -->
# # Bug
# Output of `python -c "import pydantic.utils; print(pydantic.utils.version_info())"`:
# Hey there!
# `NotRequired` is a relatively new feature for TypedDict, available in typing_extensions. (Check out [PEP 0655](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0655/).) You basically just wrap a type with NotRequired and it means "this property could either be _this type_, or it could not be present on the diction
